chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P. No.D-3913 of 2022 ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Date Order with signature of Judge ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 19.07.2022. Mr. Abdul Jalil Khan, Advocate for the Petitioners. Mr. Saifullah, Assistant, Advocate General, Sindh alongwith M/s. Abdullah Hanjrah and Sarmad Sarwar, Law Officers, ECP. Mr. Abdul Wahab Balouch, DAG. ------- This Petition involves a common question regarding rejection of Nomination Papers filed by the Petitioners in respect of the forthcoming Local Government Elections to be held on 24.7.2022. The Nominations in question were rejected by the Returning Officer on the ground that either the Proposer(s) or the Seconder(s), as the case may be, are not registered as voters in the same Electoral area or Ward from which the Petitioners are contesting. None of the Petitioners have availed the statutory remedy of Appeal as provided to them. Be that as it may, it is their case that such defect, if any, was curable, hence, the Returning Officer in each case ought to have permitted them to cure the same. As it transpires, the issue has already been decided by a learned Division Bench of this court vide order dated 30.06.2022 in C. P. No. D-3990 of 2022 and connected matters, where with reference to the judgment of the Honourable Supreme Court of Pakistan in the case reported as Rana Muhammad Tajammal Hussain V/s. Rana Shaukat Mahmood (PLD 2007 Supreme Court 277) where it was held that the defect is of a substantial and incurable nature, hence could not be permitted to be cured by either the Returning Officer, the Appellate Authority or this Court.

D-3990 of 2022 and connected matters, where with reference to the judgment of the Honourable Supreme Court of Pakistan in the case reported as Rana Muhammad Tajammal Hussain V/s. Rana Shaukat Mahmood (PLD 2007 Supreme Court 277) where it was held that the defect is of a substantial and incurable nature, hence could not be permitted to be cured by either the Returning Officer, the Appellate Authority or this Court. That being so the Petition stands dismissed accordingly. JUDGE JUDGE MUBASHIR
